plainEnglish: Color can't be the only way you communicate something. If links are set apart from text only by color, required fields are marked only in red, or a chart's series differ only by hue, then colorblind users and people in high-contrast modes miss the message. Add a second cue: an underline, an icon, text, or a pattern.
whyItMatters: Roughly 1 in 12 men is colorblind and can't tell, for example, a red 'error' field from a normal one when color is the only signal.
quickCheck: View the page in grayscale (Chrome DevTools > Rendering > Emulate vision deficiencies > Achromatopsia) and confirm links, states, and meanings are still distinguishable.
commonMistakes: Removing link underlines so links differ from body text by color alone (axe's link-in-text-block rule flags this).
Marking required or invalid form fields only with red, with no icon or text.
Charts and graphs whose data series are separable only by color, with no labels or patterns.
Showing status (online/offline, pass/fail) with a colored dot and no accompanying text.

codeExamples.label: Error conveyed with an icon and text, not just color
codeExamples.code: ⚠ Error: enter a valid email address.

codeExamples.label: Error conveyed by color alone
codeExamples.code: Enter a valid email address.
